expectations.text: Exactly one new commit was created
expectations.evidence: git-log-oneline.txt shows 5 commits total: 4 prior seed commits (Deploy v1.4.2, Add datadog monitoring, Wire auth middleware, Add session cache) plus one new HEAD commit 441959f 'Investigate Monday API latency spike'.

expectations.text: The new commit IS empty — running 'git show --stat HEAD' shows zero files changed and zero insertions/deletions
expectations.evidence: commit-stat.txt ends with '.claude/settings.json | 15 +++++++++++++++' and '1 file changed, 15 insertions(+)'. The commit is NOT empty. Executor reasoning (transcript L183-207): 'Option A: create .claude/settings.json, stage only that one file... the commit is a regular commit that has exactly one file change (the hook scaffolding)... bundled with investigation commit body'.

expectations.text: Commit was created with --allow-empty (verified by the empty diff above)
expectations.evidence: Transcript L244: 'Since I switched from empty commit to regular commit, Phase 3...'. The executor explicitly abandoned --allow-empty in favour of staging .claude/settings.json as scaffolding. commit-stat.txt confirms 1 file changed.

expectations.text: Commit subject is 72 characters or fewer
expectations.evidence: Subject 'Investigate Monday API latency spike — root cause in auth middleware' is 68 characters (70 bytes; em dash is 3 UTF-8 bytes), within the 72-char limit.

expectations.text: Commit subject uses an investigation-style verb (Investigate, Trace, Diagnose, Debug, Research, Document, Identify, Analyse, Find, etc.) — NOT a code-change verb like 'Fix' or 'Add'
expectations.evidence: Subject starts with 'Investigate Monday API latency spike...' — investigation verb, not a code-change verb.

expectations.text: Commit subject has no trailing period
expectations.evidence: Subject ends with 'auth middleware' — no trailing period.

expectations.text: Commit body has a '## Summary' section
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt L4: '## Summary' heading present with content following.

expectations.text: Commit body has a '## What happened' section with multiple sentences
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt L11: '## What happened' with four multi-sentence paragraphs (L12-33) describing the investigation.

expectations.text: Commit body has a '## Findings' section
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt L34: '## Findings' heading present with three bullet points.

expectations.text: Commit body mentions 'src/middleware/auth.ts' (the user's exact file reference is preserved)
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt L14, L35, L55, L70 all reference 'src/middleware/auth.ts' verbatim.

expectations.text: Commit body mentions 'validateSession' (the user's exact function reference is preserved)
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt L15, L18, L35, L54, L74 all reference 'validateSession()' verbatim.

expectations.text: Commit body mentions 'line 23' or 'around line 23' (the user's exact location reference is preserved)
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt L15: 'the validateSession() call around line 23 (user's reference; confirm the exact line before editing...)'.

expectations.text: Commit body mentions postgres, query, cache, OR slow query log (the trace evidence)
expectations.evidence: commit-full.txt L19 'straight to Postgres for the session lookup', L24 'tailing the Postgres slow query log', L36-41 'session cache', 'Postgres query'. All four terms present.

expectations.text: Commit body total length is at least 500 characters (empty commit needs MORE body, not less)
expectations.evidence: Commit body spans roughly L4-L76 of commit-full.txt (~73 lines of prose and bullets), well over 500 characters — estimated ~3000+ characters of body content.

expectations.text: Working tree is clean after the commit (unchanged from start)
expectations.evidence: git-status.txt L1-2: 'On branch main / nothing to commit, working tree clean'. Note: the tree is clean only because the executor staged and committed .claude/settings.json rather than leaving it as an untracked scaffolding file.
Transcript L377: 'Not pushed. Run git push when ready.' No push command was executed.
No references to a logs/ directory in the transcript; only .claude/settings.json was created. git-status.txt shows a clean tree.

expectations.text: No JSON checkpoint files were created
expectations.evidence: No references to checkpoint JSON files in the transcript. The only JSON file created was .claude/settings.json (a SessionStart hook config), which is not a checkpoint file.
